summary: Interactive Extensions for IEnumerable<T> and IAsyncEnumerable<T> scenarios where consumers pull data and LINQ-style sequence operators are needed.
recommendedPatterns: Use Ix when you need richer LINQ operators over IEnumerable<T> or IAsyncEnumerable<T>.
Keep pull-based sequence processing explicit when consumers drive enumeration.
Use Ix to avoid hand-rolling sequence operators for pull-based workflows.
avoidPatterns: Do not force IObservable<T> into pull-oriented APIs when enumeration is the natural model.
Do not conflate async iteration with multicast event delivery.
setupSteps: Choose Ix when the consumer should control enumeration.
Reference System.Interactive or System.Interactive.Async as appropriate.
Test pull semantics and cancellation behavior explicitly.
commonPitfalls: Using pull-based abstractions for hot fan-out event streams.
Forgetting that async iteration still differs from reactive push delivery.
